tickets.title: Build the isolated Baton distribution and migration architecture
tickets.objective: Convert Baton into the Baton source product and produce fail-closed new-project and mature-adoption payloads whose complete control plane lives under .baton.
scope: Rename active product identity, commands, metadata, documentation, and generated consumer content to Baton while preserving historical release evidence.
Give the Baton source repository its own root .baton control plane that is always source-only and never enters consumer payloads.
Create a separate consumer source tree and generate exact new-project and adoption payload manifests from it.
Move consumer metadata, runtime, state, dashboard, governance, roles, schemas, presets, decisions, PRDs, tickets, reviews, and reports under .baton.
Replace the packages/consumer source nesting with the conventional template/.baton layout.
Remove redundant source examples and consolidate source lifecycle utilities under scripts/.
Merge release policy into the single releasing guide.
Limit consumer files outside .baton to a managed AGENTS.md block, project Codex configuration, and repository skill-discovery links.
Replace root installed lifecycle tools with .baton/bin/baton and namespaced implementation modules.
Implement safe v0.2 through v0.5 migration, mature-state quarantine, project-version separation, ignored/vendor-safe validation, backups, rollback, and human-approved cleanup candidates.
Convert the GitHub repository from template mode to a normal repository without publishing a new release.
Keep docs exclusively user/contributor-facing by moving evaluator specifications to tests/evals.
Remove the root release directory and keep release construction under scripts/.
Remove the repository-wide source-classification inventory and express only consumer payload projection exceptions in release code.
nonGoals: Publish, tag, or release the candidate.
Rename or mutate consuming project identity, licensing, community, source, test, example, release, or documentation files.
Automatically parse mature Markdown records into authoritative state without explicit validated integration.
Automatically delete legacy v0.2 through v0.5 installed files or transaction backups.
affectedSystems: Status, update, migration, backup, and rollback
Codex AGENTS, custom-agent, configuration, and skill discovery
Baton operational state and generated dashboard
acceptanceCriteria: The active product is named Baton and the source repository is not configured as a GitHub template.
The source repository root .baton contains its own live project state and is structurally excluded from consumer payloads.
Consumer .baton content is sourced only from template/.baton and cannot include the source repository's root .baton.
The obsolete packages/consumer, packages, examples, tools, root install.sh, and separate release-policy document paths are absent.
Source installer, evaluator, and release builder implementations live under scripts/ while release consumers still receive the top-level install.sh asset.

Release construction is rooted exclusively at template/.baton, rejects every source outside that boundary, treats template content as shared by default, and encodes only explicit new-project/adoption projection exceptions.
New-project and existing-project payload manifests are separate, exact, checksum-bound, and validated.
Existing-project adoption never adds or replaces root project identity, legal/community, GitHub, docs, examples, tests, tools, release, evaluator, or installer files.
Consumer status and update run through .baton/bin/baton and no root install.sh is installed.
Outside .baton, Baton touches only an idempotent AGENTS.md block, project Codex configuration, and individual .agents/skills links, preserving every collision.
Baton version comes only from Baton metadata and immutable provenance; a project's VERSION or other version source remains optional and project-owned.

New Baton installations create only .baton/metadata.json; legacy .agent-harness.json is read for migration, preserved, and reported as a human-approved cleanup candidate rather than created or deleted automatically.
Mature adoption quarantines starter state until a complete schema-valid migration is explicitly approved and activated.
Baton source evaluation runs only in the product repository; the consumer validator inspects only Baton-owned or explicitly linked Git-visible non-ignored paths.

Supported v0.2 through v0.5 migration preserves every legacy path; schemas without per-file baselines produce immutable comparison evidence without guessed project-path candidates, while only checksum-matching legacy managed/generated paths become human-approved cleanup candidates.
Rollback restores every touched project path and reports external backup and transaction locations.

A representative mature-repository adoption fixture preserves VERSION, LICENSE, .github, tests, tools, docs, existing governance, and unrelated dirty work while producing no ignored-vendor false failures.
No release, tag, or publication occurs before a later explicit human instruction.
docs contains only public product and contributor documentation; evaluator prompts, rubrics, schemas, and scenarios live under tests/evals.
The root release directory and scripts/source-classification.json are absent; exact generated release manifests remain the auditable payload inventory.
requiredVerification: Static template-boundary projection and dual-manifest validation
Fresh new-project install smoke
Mature non-empty adoption smoke with forbidden root paths
v0.2, v0.3, v0.4, and v0.5 migration smokes
Status, update, collision, rollback, symlink, unreadable-target, and concurrent-lock smokes
Codex AGENTS, custom-agent, config, and skill-discovery checks
Ignored root and nested vendor traversal regression smoke
Canonical-state quarantine, migration, dashboard, team, assurance, and lifecycle smokes
Python 3.9 and current Python compatibility runs
Fresh-folder smoke outside the source repository
Independent two-axis review and disposable Internal Audit
expectedEvidence: Exact template projection with no source-repository path eligible for either payload
Exact new-project and adoption payload path lists and checksums
Before-and-after migration manifests proving no automatic deletion and distinguishing baseline-backed cleanup candidates from preserved no-baseline paths
Representative mature-repository snapshots proving forbidden paths and existing files are unchanged
Passing deterministic and interactive smoke output on supported Python versions
Implementation report with limitations, rollback evidence, and remaining release decision
Independent review findings and Internal Audit verdict
risks: A high-fan-out rename can leave compatibility aliases or documentation inconsistent.
Separating source and consumer layouts can break immutable update-baseline reconstruction.
AGENTS.md and Codex config integration can corrupt user formatting or duplicate managed blocks if not idempotent.
Quarantined mature state can accidentally become authoritative before explicit activation.
Legacy cleanup automation can delete project-owned files unless retirement is changed to human-approved candidates.
Source-repository files can leak into payloads unless the builder rejects every input outside template/.baton and validates exact archive manifests.

tickets.title: Build Management-first bootstrap and project-local company memory
tickets.objective: Implement the approved bootstrap, named-workforce, review, project-local memory, privacy, and bounded-context experience without turning Baton into a game or a second project-management authority.
scope: Add Management-first provider-aware bootstrap with task registration, interruption recovery, and copy-prompt fallback.
Add stable named personnel identities, bounded professional working styles, employment history, reviews, reuse, firing, rehiring, and user-approved permanent-seat replacement.
Add one project-local memory root with schema-versioned memory.json, history.jsonl, confirmation, forgetting, privacy, migrations, and transactional rollback.
Add one memory skill and role-specific on-demand context selection capped at approximately 10 claims or 600 automatic memory tokens.
Integrate installer, adoption, update, dashboard, evaluator, tests, and documentation.
nonGoals: Create a game, employee leaderboard, forced rotation, novelty quota, or weaker-worker selection.
Create global memory, imports, cross-project synchronization, raw transcript storage, or automatic Git-history rewriting.
Duplicate project decisions, tickets, approvals, PRDs, or implementation evidence in memory.
Begin implementation before normal readiness and post-v0.6 release-boundary resolution.
affectedSystems: Consumer schemas, memory, personnel, and migrations
Team, task registry, roles, and lifecycle
Installer, adoption, activation, update, backups, and rollback
Static evaluator, deterministic tests, smokes, and documentation
acceptanceCriteria: Bootstrap creates Management first, registers real permanent tasks idempotently, supports unavailable task tools, and gates delivery on required wake paths.
Every coworker has a stable editable name, bounded professional working style, reusable history, and evidence-backed contextual performance.
One project-local memory stores confirmed company, user, and personnel knowledge with no global import or synchronization.
Personal inference requires confirmation; verified company and coworker events may be automatic; secrets and sensitive data are rejected by default.
Forgetting removes current values, redacts local memory history, and warns about Git retention without rewriting Git history.
One memory skill provides the complete user-facing interface while deterministic tooling validates authority, schema, transactions, and rollback.
Automatic memory briefings are role-specific and remain within the agreed claim/token cap.
Permanent-seat replacement requires explicit user approval and no forced coworker rotation or exploration quota exists.
The feature remains generic and preserves fresh-install, mature-adoption, update, collision, rollback, and release safety.
requiredVerification: Memory, personnel, history-event, authority, migration, and cross-record schema tests
Bootstrap forward tests with task tools available, unavailable, interrupted, and already complete
Identity, duplicate prevention, review, reuse, firing, replacement, and rehire fixtures
Candidate confirmation, secret rejection, forgetting, history redaction, and Git-warning fixtures
Exact role-specific context-budget tests
Local, piped, interactive, fresh, mature-adoption, update, rollback, Python, and remote release smokes
Independent two-axis review, Product Designer gates, and disposable Internal Audit
expectedEvidence: Approved technical design and Product Designer readiness packet
Exact context packets for Management, Operations, Consultants, and Contractors
Evidence-backed personnel selection and replacement histories
Implementation report with full deterministic and runtime proof
risks: Excess memory context can reduce agent performance.
Unconfirmed personal inference can become a harmful assumption.
Performance history can become self-reinforcing without visible evidence and user correction.
Personality can become gimmicky or reduce quality if it exceeds bounded professional style.
Task creation can duplicate permanent roles without real identity and idempotency.
Privacy expectations can conflict with JSONL and Git retention.
Schema or migration failure can damage durable company history.

tickets.title: Design the bootstrap and memory module boundary
tickets.objective: Compare contrasting evidence-backed interfaces for Baton's bootstrap, personnel memory, history, task registration, and bounded recall before selecting the implementation seam.
scope: Inspect current consumer runtime, schemas, state, skills, installer projections, update migrations, dashboard, and tests.
Produce at least three independent designs with explicit interfaces, invariants, failure behavior, migration behavior, and deletion-test leverage.
Recommend one architecture and record rejected alternatives without implementing the feature.
nonGoals: Implement schemas, runtime, skills, installer changes, dashboard changes, or migrations.
Publish, tag, or modify the frozen v0.6 candidate branch.
Expand the confirmed product contract or create new user-facing CLI commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Three contrasting independent proposals are grounded in the live repository.
The recommendation defines a small stable interface, storage authority, locking, transactions, migrations, privacy, task capability fallback, and context-budget behavior.
The deletion test and rejected alternatives show why the selected module is deep rather than a pass-through wrapper.
No feature implementation or publication occurs in the investigation.
requiredVerification: Read-only inspection of targeted callers, schemas, release projections, and tests
Comparison against the confirmed BATON-002 PRD
State and link checks after the decision record is integrated
expectedEvidence: Operations recommendation with explicit implementation seam and unresolved Product Designer inputs
risks: A storage-first design can leak JSONL and migration mechanics through skill contracts.
A task-provider adapter can imply capabilities unavailable on some Codex surfaces.
A broad memory module can become a second project-management authority.
An underspecified transaction boundary can corrupt current memory and append-only history together.

tickets.title: Define Product Designer readiness for bootstrap and memory
tickets.objective: Translate the confirmed BATON-002 outcome and accepted architecture into explicit, accessible interaction requirements for bootstrap, interruption recovery, task fallback, memory control, personnel presentation, and dashboard comprehension.
scope: Define the Management-first onboarding journey and one-question-at-a-time project discovery.
Define complete, interrupted, resumed, task-tools-unavailable, mature-adoption, and privacy-sensitive states.
Define copy-ready fallback prompts, task-online feedback, delivery gating, memory inspect/correct/forget/candidate flows, and repository-sharing warnings.
Define dashboard information hierarchy, safe personnel/history projection, keyboard behavior, accessibility, and mobile/desktop acceptance.
nonGoals: Implement the memory engine, skills, task creation, dashboard, installer, or tests.
Change the confirmed product outcome, authority model, technical architecture, or public CLI.
Approve technical integration, publication, or Product Designer acceptance evidence.
affectedSystems: Task capability fallback and interruption recovery
acceptanceCriteria: The target user, entry point, full journey, decision points, content, states, recovery behavior, and completion boundary are explicit.
Every task-capability state communicates what is online, awaiting user action, or delivery-blocking without exposing unnecessary agent mechanics.
Remember, inspect, confirm, correct, forget, candidate-review, and Git-retention behavior are understandable and reversible.
The light theatrical tone ends after onboarding and never obscures authority, privacy, errors, or recovery.
Keyboard, focus, contrast, screen-reader labeling, responsive layout, and reduced-motion expectations are testable.
The packet defines representative evidence required for later Product Designer acceptance.
requiredVerification: Review against the BATON-002 PRD and accepted architecture decision
State-by-state normal, failure, interruption, privacy, and mature-adoption walkthrough
Product Designer readiness verdict with exact evidence path
expectedEvidence: Approved Product Designer Readiness review on BATON-002
No implementation changes during the readiness definition
risks: Role-play can become distracting or obscure actionable status.
Task fallback can look successful before a valid wake path exists.
Dashboard summaries can leak private claims or create a worker leaderboard.
Forgetting can overpromise deletion from Git or external backups.
Dense memory controls can exceed the simple one-skill interaction goal.
